stem: In a right triangle, the angle of measure x degrees is acute. The side opposite this angle has length 5 units and the hypotenuse has length 13 units. What is the value of the sine of x degrees?
rationale:
Choice B is correct. The sine of an acute angle in a right triangle is the ratio of the length of the side opposite the angle to the length of the hypotenuse, which is 5 over 13.

Choice A is incorrect: 1 over 13 has no special trigonometric meaning here.

Choice C is incorrect: 12 over 13 is the cosine of x degrees.

Choice D is incorrect: 13 over 5 is the reciprocal of the sine.
